scenarios.title: LLM Post-Training and RLHF Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: SFT, LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, DPO, PPO, KD, QAT, GRPO, RLHF utilities, reward/log-prob helpers, post-training configs, and preference optimization tasks.
scenarios.role: Guides torchtune's PyTorch-native LLM post-training recipes, configs, RLHF utilities, and safe command planning.
scenarios.read_when: The request names torchtune, tune run, LoRA, QLoRA, DPO, PPO, KD, QAT, GRPO, recipe configs, checkpoint resume, or torchtune.rlhf.
scenarios.best_for: Selecting and adapting torchtune post-training recipes, validating configs/data, planning distributed launches, and debugging torchtune-specific post-training failures.
scenarios.avoid_when: The request is about a different training framework such as Axolotl, LlamaFactory, TRL, OpenRLHF, or generic Transformers APIs with no torchtune-specific surface.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose torchtune when the workflow is explicitly `tune` CLI, torchtune recipe/config, PyTorch-native post-training, or torchtune RLHF utility work; choose neighboring RLHF/training skills when their package owns the runtime.

scenarios.title: Distributed PyTorch Training and Large Model Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Distributed PyTorch training, torchrun launchers, checkpointing, FSDP-style memory planning, dtype/device decisions, and large-model training operations.
scenarios.role: Explains torchtune's distributed recipe launch shape, torchrun argument placement, checkpointing, precision, memory, and runtime preflight helpers.
scenarios.read_when: The request mentions torchtune distributed recipes, `--nproc_per_node`, `--nnodes`, multinode, checkpoints, bf16, activation offloading, optimizer-in-backward, or memory planning.
scenarios.best_for: Building safe torchtune distributed commands and diagnosing torchtune-specific launcher/checkpoint/device errors before expensive jobs.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is generic PyTorch distributed programming or a non-torchtune launcher/framework.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use torchtune for `tune run` distributed recipe operations; use broader distributed PyTorch skills for framework-agnostic process group or custom loop development.

scenarios.title: LLM Fine-Tuning, Training, and Serving Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: LLM fine-tuning, model/tokenizer config wiring, local generation, evaluation, quantization, adapter handling, and checkpoint-to-inference flows.
scenarios.role: Covers torchtune model/dataset/config wiring plus generation, Eleuther evaluation, quantization, and adapter/checkpoint routing after training.
scenarios.read_when: The request names torchtune models, tokenizers, adapters, generation, Eleuther evaluation, quantize, `FullModelTorchTuneCheckpointer`, or checkpoint output folders.
scenarios.best_for: Using torchtune-specific model builders, PEFT modules, data transforms, generation/evaluation/quantization recipes, and checkpoint compatibility guidance.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is serving an OpenAI-compatible endpoint, using vLLM/SGLang directly, or generic Transformers generation with no torchtune config/checkpoint layer.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose torchtune when model/data/eval/quantization work depends on torchtune `_component_` dotpaths, recipe configs, or checkpoint conventions.

scenarios.title: Language Model Evaluation Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: LLM benchmark configuration, EleutherAI Eval Harness tasks, evaluation configs, task limits, result planning, and evaluation optional dependencies.
scenarios.role: Guides torchtune's `eleuther_eval` recipe, evaluation config changes, optional `lm_eval` dependency, and checkpoint compatibility.
scenarios.read_when: The request mentions torchtune Eleuther evaluation, `eleuther_evaluation`, `lm_eval`, task lists, `truthfulqa_mc2`, or evaluating a torchtune checkpoint.
scenarios.best_for: Planning torchtune evaluation commands and config edits after a compatible checkpoint/tokenizer exists.
scenarios.avoid_when: The request is about writing custom Eleuther tasks or using lm-evaluation-harness directly outside torchtune.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use torchtune for evaluation routed through `tune run eleuther_eval`; use an lm-evaluation-harness skill for harness-native task authoring or backend work.

scenarios.title: Data, Model, Registry, and Template Customization
scenarios.when_to_read: Dataset schemas, message rows, column mappings, model/tokenizer registries, prompt templates, custom components, and config `_component_` customization.
scenarios.role: Explains torchtune dataset/message transforms, JSONL validation, config component dotpaths, model builders, tokenizer choices, and public API boundaries.
scenarios.read_when: The request mentions torchtune datasets, Message rows, ShareGPT/OpenAI/Alpaca transforms, `column_map`, packing, prompt templates, model builders, or `_component_` dotpaths.
scenarios.best_for: Adapting torchtune data/model config components and validating small data fixtures before a recipe run.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is generic dataset conversion unrelated to torchtune recipes or a different ML framework's registry.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose torchtune when the data/model customization must fit torchtune builders, message transforms, or recipe YAML component fields.
